title: campanula sibirica subsp. sibirica
intro: Campanula sibirica subsp. sibirica, also known as Siberian bellflower, is a perennial plant species native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia, characterized by its blue-purple, bell-shaped flowers. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving stress, and boosting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Campanula sibirica subsp. sibirica are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it as a treatment for any medical condition.
